title: Who's Coaching the Coach?
description: For this week’s Saturday Blueprint, we revisit timely and timeless lessons to help us coach ourselves and others toward growth and deeper connection in the days ahead.
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Rule of Thirds: Olympian Alexi Pappas on Chasing Dreams & Embracing Crappy Days
description: Don’t let the mind bully the body into quitting on the crappy days.
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Cost of a Curated Life: Cultivating Healthy Habits
description: Our ability to lead begins with our ability to show up for ourselves with deeper conviction and consistency.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking ― 2.23.25
description: “Prepare yourself so you can be a rainbow in someone else's cloud.”
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Messy, Beautiful Truth About Resilience with Dr. Becky Kennedy
description: “What does resilience feel like?”
author: The Daily Coach

title: 'Do Your Job: The Art of Winning' with Bill Belichick
description: In his upcoming book “The Art of Winning,” set to be released in May, Coach Bill Belichick shares several key secrets to his extraordinary success on the field.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking ― 2.16.25
description: "May you maintain your soft, beautiful heart, but may you also deeply know your worth."
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder ― Forging Your Own Path
description: We’re all put in front of paths never before traveled. Everyone has to be the pioneer, the first explorer, in their own story.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder ― 1.31.25
description: You were born for something significant—something only you can bring into the world.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder ― 1.24.25
description: Every setback offers two paths: to see it as a defeat or to embrace it as a lesson. The choice is always ours.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Saturday Blueprint With Michael Lombardi
description: We spoke to Daily Coach co-founder and UNC Football’s newest General Manager about assessing teams, evaluating talent within organizations, and building a championship team culture.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder ― 1.17.25
description: Never let a position or title threaten you into being less than who you should be as a person.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Dear 2024: Reflections on Leadership and Life
description: Your lessons will linger far beyond your calendar days. They will shape how I lead, love, and live in the days ahead.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking ― 1.5.25
description: "You don't have to be perfect to help people. All you do is have to be real."
author: The Daily Coach

title: 2024 Saturday Blueprint Recap: A Year of Riveting Stories and Leadership Gems
description: As we close out 2024, we’ve compiled a selection of thought-provoking quotes from this year’s Saturday Blueprint series.
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Daily Coach's 10 Most-Read Pieces of 2024
description: Thank you for being part of our journey and allowing us to be part of yours.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking ― 12.29.24
description: "Remember, when a door closes. It's not rejection but divine protection."
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking ― 12.22.24
description: "When someone shows you who they are, believe them the first time."
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Pareto Principle in Practice: Why Two Is Better Than Three
description: By leveraging the Pareto Principle and following these five steps, we can avoid analysis paralysis and concentrate on decisions that truly move the needle.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder ― 12.27.24
description: Life is too precious to be lived on autopilot.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder ― 12.20.24
description: In essence, we all wear an invisible mask that silently communicates a universal message: “Make me feel important."
author: The Daily Coach

title: Reclaiming Hope: The Gift That Holds Us Up
description: Hope is a gift that holds us up, even when life lets us down.
author: The Daily Coach

title: A Servant’s Heart: Creating Leaders, Not Followers
author: The Daily Coach

title: Insights on Drive, Sleep, Fulfillment, Energy, and Culture
description: We put together some of our favorite quotes from our guests about staying driven and sustaining excellence, improving the quality of our sleep, and redefining success and fulfillment.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder ― 12.13.24
description: If you're not managing your thoughts, you're leaving a lot of happiness and success on the table.
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Army-Navy Game: Leadership Lessons from a Historic Rivalry
description: Leaders can apply the principles demonstrated in the Army-Navy Game to enhance their leadership skills and effectiveness.
author: The Daily Coach

title: 'Everybody’s Job Is Everybody’s Job': Leadership, Mortality, and Meaning
description: The reality is our time to live, lead, love, and experience this journey called life is finite, precious, and limited.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Rooted in Substance: Timeless Lessons from Oregon’s Dan Lanning
description: The climb is never easy, but as Dan Lanning's story reminds us, true transformation lies in embracing the process and finding a way through the obstacles.
author: The Daily Coach

title: How Much Strategy, How Much Leadership?
description: Leadership can survive without a great strategy. Strategy without leadership cannot.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking ― 12.8.24
description: "What you do frequently becomes your frequency."
author: The Daily Coach

title: How Great Leaders Deliver "Unreasonable Hospitality"
description: Some may believe that leadership doesn’t involve hospitality, but that couldn’t be further from the truth.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder ― 12.6.24
description: It’s natural to feel tested by life's challenges, but it’s essential to reframe these challenges as opportunities for growth.
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Gratitude Shift: From 'Grateful For' to 'Grateful In'
description: For many of us, 2024 has been a year of extremes. The highs have felt exhilarating, and the lows have been profoundly difficult.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "The universe meets you at the depth of your surrender―not at the height of your struggle."
author: The Daily Coach

title: 'It Always Begins Before It Begins': Lessons in Preparation and Selflessness
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder
description: We don't have to wait for special occasions to convey to people around us that we're grateful for their presence in our lives.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Leadership Lessons from a Locker Room Serenade
description: Leadership is about more than strategy, vision, and execution—it’s about humanity.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "People who double down on kindness even when life has been rough bring a special magic into the world."
author: The Daily Coach

title: Doing the Right Thing: The Shopping Cart Test
description: Returning the cart is a small act of kindness, a sign of consideration, and a willingness to take a few extra seconds to do what’s right.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Leadership on Loan: When to Lead, When to Let Go
description: This idea of “renting” a role provides a valuable perspective on leadership and letting go: we don’t own our positions; we steward them temporarily.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "Trust the timing that people enter your life, but also trust the timing in which people leave."
author: The Daily Coach

title: Honor the Past
description: Once the Patriots embrace their past, stop taking credit for the past success and honor those that helped win six Super Bowls, they can forge into their future.
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Saban Shift: From Transactional to Transformational Leadership
description: As coaches, executives, educators and decision-makers, it’s essential to understand the key differences between transactional and transformational leadership.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "If anyone is magically going to appear and suddenly make your life better, just know that person is always going to be you."
author: The Daily Coach

title: Leadership Lessons from UConn's Paige Bueckers' "Summer World Tour"
description: There is no substitute for hard work and commitment to the process and the craft.
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Interdisciplinary Edge: Joe Mazzulla's Approach to Championship-Level Leadership
description: By adopting an interdisciplinary mindset and leveraging the strength of professional networks, we as leaders can stay ahead of the curve.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder
description: Every day in our lives is packed with insurmountable opportunities for personal growth.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Level 5 Leadership: The Journey from Good to Great
description: The real question is whether you have the resolve and resilience to become a more effective and transformational leader.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "You don't need to have all the answers right now. You just need to focus on the next small step in front of you."
author: The Daily Coach

title: Winston's Star: A Playbook for Impactful Communication
description: Winston’s Star lays out five key elements to make presentations memorable and impactful.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "Hold on to your softness. This world will give you every reason to harden. Don't let it steal the beautiful parts of you that make you human."
author: The Daily Coach

title: When Our Impulses Aren't Right
description: We often benefit more having truth-tellers who can logically push back against our worst impulses.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "Everybody deserves to eat. Not everybody deserves to eat at your table."
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Willingness to Endure
description: We can only achieve endurance through a willingness to embrace hardship, to never let a setback destroy our day.
author: The Daily Coach

title: "Notes" of An Elder
description: Everybody wants to be paid, but not everybody wants to do the work.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "If you don't know what to pursue in life right now ― pursue yourself."
author: The Daily Coach

title: Leading with 'Unreasonable Hospitality'
description: At the core of a connection lies the courage to lead with vulnerability and honesty.
author: The Daily Coach

title: 5 Keys to Embracing Discomfort
description: Two-percenters get more done because they instinctively choose hard over easy — in every aspect of their life.
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Danger of Worrying About Results
description: In any competitive setting, outliers will occur, and the result will not always be what we had hoped.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: “The universe buries strange jewels deep within us all, and then stands back to see if we can find them.”
author: The Daily Coach

title: The Urgency Mindset: What Are You Willing to Tolerate?
description: Urgency is a key skill and a driving force behind sustained excellence and high performance.
author: The Daily Coach

title: When Problems Arise
description: Problems don’t have to be viewed as a headache every time they come up. Often, they're opportunities to showcase our real leadership prowess.

title: Sunday Thinking
description: “The most beautiful things in life are not just things. They're people and places, memories and pictures. They're feelings and moments and smiles and laughter.”

title: The Music in Miami
description: Our short and long-term success will be determined by whether we can productively push ourselves and those we lead beyond our natural comfort zones.

title: "Notes" of An Elder
description: Everyone needs a mentor regardless of their title, position, wealth, or success.
author: The Daily Coach

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "I hope you learn how to be proud of where you are — even if it isn't exactly where you want to be."

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "Be obsessed with your opportunity."

title: Steve Kerr and the Quest for 'A Damn Gold Medal'
description: The criticism of Team USA Basketball Coach Steve Kerr despite the team's Olympic success thus far has three important leadership reminders.

title: Sunday Thinking
description: "Embrace wanting to risk your heart. It is one of the greatest things you will ever do."
author: The Daily Coach
